pi_items.source: setup_task.sh script explicitly generates a memo reducing the 'Client Entertainment' expense limit to $100.
setup_task.sh script generates a memo discontinuing the 'Telecom & Internet' category, meaning its active status should be set to 0.

setup_task.sh script details 3 pending claims for David Kim: an $85 Client Entertainment claim (Approved since <= $100), a $120 Client Entertainment claim (Rejected since > $100), and a $45 Telecom & Internet claim (Rejected since category is discontinued).

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a synthetically generated memo (q3_austerity_memo.txt) created by setup_task.sh. The memo explicitly reduces the 'Client Entertainment' limit to $100.00 and fully discontinues the 'Telecom & Internet' category. It also lists three specific pending claims for David Kim (EMP005): an $85 Client Entertainment claim (which should be Approved as it is under the new limit), a $120 Client Entertainment claim (which must be Rejected for exceeding the limit), and a $45 Telecom & Internet claim (which must be Rejected due to the category being discontinued).
